data.body:
LANDING ATTESTATION + COMPLETION EVIDENCE (2026-07-18)

AC7 [operator-attested] — landed on main through Gerrit, two-vote gate green, 0 unresolved:
- Change #781 "R2 eval: pre-register + self-gate the operator-attested-evidence DET detector" — MERGED to main; merge commit a67f85f3c542148d13cd031fc21d55c05589e371; LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1; 0 unresolved comments.
- Change #782 "R2: activate the operator-attested-evidence advisory DET lint in p6" — MERGED to main; merge commit 1c28d5ae081874c77ff70d091a1730dc3676f1ae; LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1; 0 unresolved comments.
- Both merge commits are ancestors of origin/main (git merge-base --is-ancestor confirms). The stack preserves git-ancestry for AC1: #781 (eval) is the parent of #782 (activation), so the deterministic self-gate eval (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/operator_attested_eval.json: precision 92.2% on a 64-item flagged census, flag rate 2.07%, recall 61.3%, verdict PASS) was committed BEFORE the lint was activated in p6.
- CI Verified +1 on both changes == `make lint && make typecheck && make test` passed on GitHub Actions (AC6).

AC4 (single-source [operator-attested] tag matcher) — ownership moved det_floor -> det_operator_attested, intent preserved:
- The AC text names `det_floor` as the owner of `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE`. During landing, CI's module-size ratchet (.github/module-size-ceilings.txt pins det_floor.py at 975 LOC) failed because adding the lint grew det_floor past its ceiling. Per the module-size policy (split along call-graph seams; never grow a grandfathered file), the entire operator-attested lint — INCLUDING `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE` — was extracted into a NEW module `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py` (176 LOC, pure leaf), returning det_floor to 971 LOC (<= 975).
- The single-source INTENT of AC4 is fully satisfied: the plan-time lint and the completion-verifier enrichment share ONE regex object. `det_operator_attested._OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE is workflow_ops._OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE` returns True (workflow_ops imports it as a re-export). Unit tests `test_tag_regex_single_source` and `test_agreement_with_workflow_ops` (tests/unit/test_det_floor_operator_attested.py) assert the identity and the agreement.
- Corrected proof command (owner is det_operator_attested, not det_floor): `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.det_operator_attested import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE as a; from rebar.llm.plan_review.workflow_ops import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE as b; assert a is b"` exits 0.
- This is an implementation-location change forced by a CI constraint (module-size ratchet); the requirement's substance — a single shared tag matcher so the lint and the completion verifier agree on "tagged" by construction — is met. (The det_floor references in AC3/AC5 are unaffected: DET_CHECKS stays in det_floor at exactly 9, validate_packaged_routing() == [], and docs/plan-review-gate.md documents the lint.)
